mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect consumer spending strength that directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to positively correlate with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% indicates severe labor market slack that depresses aggregate income and consumer spending, forcing downward earnings revisions and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer prices alongside deceleration in industrial production creates a stagflationary margin squeeze; this joint pattern raises real discount rates and lowers earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO adds no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 2) for 60-day forward returns, or the joint signal produces positive returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery periods, strong payroll growth signals rebounding labor income and consumer demand without immediate wage-price spirals, lifting corporate sales expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession months, PAYEMS growth fails to positively correlate with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals acute goods-sector weakness that immediately reduces trucking, energy demand, and manufacturing earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Following month-over-month INDPRO declines, the S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in housing starts drives upstream demand for construction materials, durable goods, and mortgage credit, which amplifies cyclical earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST growth exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 against 60-day forward returns in any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises short-term discount rates and reduces the present value of corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS fail to precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of occurrences during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth signals a contraction in aggregate demand that forces broad-based earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, the S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of observations.
